content:
# Mark Chapter 5 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), folios 33v–34v (PDF pages 66–68) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_005_page_001.jpg`, `MRK_005_page_002.jpg`, `MRK_005_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ And they-came to the other-side of-the sea, into the region of-the Gerasenes.

² And having-come-out of-him out-of the boat, immediately met him out-of the tombs a-man in an-unclean spirit,

³ who had his dwelling in the tombs, and not-even with-a-chain was-anyone any-longer able to-bind him,

⁴ on-account-of him many-times with-shackles and chains having-been-bound, and the chains having-been-torn-apart by him and the shackles having-been-shattered, and no-one was-strong-[enough] to-tame him.

⁵ And through-all night and day, in the tombs and in the mountains, he-was crying-out and cutting himself with stones.

⁶ And having-seen Jesus from-a-distance, he-ran and bowed-down-to him,

⁷ and having-cried-out [with] a-great voice, he-says, "What [is there] to-me and to-you, Jesus, Son of-the God Most-High? I-adjure you by God, do not torment me!"

⁸ For he-was-saying to-him, "Come-out, the unclean spirit, out-of the man!"

⁹ And he-was-asking him, "What [is] your name?" And he-says to-him, "Legion [is] my name, because we-are many."

¹⁰ And he-was-imploring him much that he-would not send them outside the region.

¹¹ And there-was there beside the mountain a-great herd of-pigs feeding.

¹² And they-implored him, saying, "Send us into the pigs, that we-may-enter into them."

¹³ And he-permitted them. And having-come-out, the unclean spirits entered into the pigs, and the herd rushed down the steep-bank into the sea — about two-thousand — and were-being-drowned in the sea.

¹⁴ And the-ones feeding them fled and reported in the city and in the countryside, and they-came to-see what it-is that has-happened.

¹⁵ And they-come to Jesus and behold the-one-being-demonized sitting, having-been-clothed and being-of-sound-mind — the-one having-had the legion — and they-were-afraid.

¹⁶ And the-ones having-seen described to-them how it-happened to-the-one-being-demonized, and about the pigs.

¹⁷ And they-began to-implore him to-go-away from their region.

¹⁸ And as-he-was-embarking into the boat, the-one having-been-demonized was-imploring him that he-might-be with him.

¹⁹ And he-did not allow him, but says to-him, "Go to your house, to your-own-[people], and report to-them how-many-things the Lord has-done for-you and [how] he-had-mercy-on you."

²⁰ And he-went-away and began to-proclaim in the Decapolis how-many-things Jesus did for-him, and all were-marveling.

²¹ And Jesus having-crossed-over in the boat again to the other-side, a-great crowd was-gathered to him, and he-was beside the sea.

²² And comes one of-the synagogue-rulers, Jairus by-name, and having-seen him, falls at his feet

²³ and implores him much, saying, "My little-daughter is at-[the]-end; [I ask] that having-come you-may-lay the hands on-her, in-order-that she-may-be-saved and may-live."

²⁴ And he-went-away with him, and a-great crowd was-following him and was-pressing-upon him.

²⁵ And a-woman being in a-flow of-blood twelve years,

²⁶ and having-suffered many-[things] under many physicians, and having-spent all the-[things] from-her, and having-been-benefited nothing but rather having-come into the worse,

²⁷ having-heard the-[things] about Jesus, having-come in the crowd from-behind, touched his garment.

²⁸ For she-was-saying, "If I-touch even his garments, I-will-be-saved."

²⁹ And immediately the fountain of her blood was-dried-up, and she-knew in-[her]-body that she-has-been-healed from the affliction.

³⁰ And immediately Jesus, having-known in himself the power having-gone-out from him, having-turned-around in the crowd, was-saying, "Who touched my garments?"

³¹ And his disciples were-saying to-him, "You-see the crowd pressing-upon you, and you-say, 'Who touched me?'"

³² And he-was-looking-around to-see the-one having-done this.

³³ And the woman, fearing and trembling, knowing what had-happened to-her, came and fell-down-before him and told him all the truth.

³⁴ And he said to-her, "Daughter, your faith has-saved you. Go in peace, and be healed from your affliction."

³⁵ While-he-was still speaking, they-come from the synagogue-ruler's [house], saying, "Your daughter died; why still trouble the teacher?"

³⁶ But Jesus, having-overheard the word being-spoken, says to-the synagogue-ruler, "Do not fear; only believe."

³⁷ And he-did not allow anyone to-accompany him except Peter and James and John the brother of-James.

³⁸ And they-come to the house of-the synagogue-ruler, and he-beholds a-commotion and [people] weeping and wailing greatly.

³⁹ And having-entered, he-says to-them, "Why do-you-make-a-commotion and weep? The child did not die but sleeps."

⁴⁰ And they-were-laughing-at him. But he, having-put-out all, takes-along the father of-the child and the mother and the-ones with him, and enters where the child was.

⁴¹ And having-grasped the hand of-the child, he-says to-her, "Talitha koum," which is being-translated, "Little-girl, to-you I-say, arise."

⁴² And immediately the little-girl arose and was-walking — for she-was twelve years [old]. And they-were-amazed immediately [with] a-great amazement.

⁴³ And he-ordered them much that no-one should-know this, and he-said to-give her [something] to-eat.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **5:1 — "Gerasenes" (ΓΕΡΑΣΗΝΩΝ):** Sinaiticus reads "Gerasenes." Vaticanus reads "Gadarenes" (ΓΑΔΑΡΗΝΩΝ). Some manuscripts read "Gergesenes." The geography is debated — Gerasa is about 30 miles from the Sea of Galilee. This is a well-known textual variant.

2. **5:9 — "Legion":** A Latin loanword (λεγιών) referring to a Roman military unit of ~5,000-6,000 soldiers. The political overtones — demonic occupation mirroring Roman occupation — may be intentional.

3. **5:23 — "is at the end" (ΕΣΧΑΤΩΣ ΕΧΕΙ):** Literally "is having [it] finally" — an idiom meaning "at the point of death."

4. **5:36 — "having overheard" (ΠΑΡΑΚΟΥΣΑΣ):** This verb can mean either "having overheard" or "having ignored/disregarded." Sinaiticus supports "overhearing." The ambiguity is significant.

5. **5:41 — "Talitha koum":** Aramaic preserved in the Greek text: טַלְיְתָא קוּמִי (ṭalyeṯā qūmī). Mark translates for his Greek-speaking audience. The preservation of Jesus's original Aramaic words is a mark of early tradition, possibly Peter's eyewitness memory.

6. **5:42 — "twelve years":** The woman's illness lasted twelve years (v. 25); the girl was twelve years old. The parallel is likely intentional.

— EMET, AI Translator
